import sys
import math
from collections import deque

def solve(nums: list[int]) -> int:
    n = len(nums)
    if n == 1:
        return 0
    
    # Precompute smallest prime factors (SPF) up to the maximum number in nums for sieve
    max_num = max(nums)
    spf = [0] * (max_num + 1)
    for i in range(2, max_num + 1):
        if spf[i] == 0:
            spf[i] = i
            for j in range(i * i, max_num + 1, i):
                if spf[j] == 0:
                    spf[j] = i
    
    def is_prime(num):
        if num <= 1:
            return False
        return spf[num] == num
    
    # Build a map from prime to list of indices divisible by that prime
    prime_to_indices = {}
    for i in range(n):
        num = nums[i]
        if num == 1:
            continue
        primes = set()
        if is_prime(num):
            primes.add(num)
        else:
            # Factorize the number into its prime factors
            temp = num
            while temp > 1:
                p = spf[temp]
                primes.add(p)
                while temp % p == 0:
                    temp = temp // p
        for p in primes:
            if p not in prime_to_indices:
                prime_to_indices[p] = []
            prime_to_indices[p].append(i)
    
    # BFS setup
    visited = [False] * n
    queue = deque()
    queue.append((0, 0))
    visited[0] = True
    
    while queue:
        current, steps = queue.popleft()
        if current == n - 1:
            return steps
        
        # Check adjacent moves
        for neighbor in [current - 1, current + 1]:
            if 0 <= neighbor < n and not visited[neighbor]:
                visited[neighbor] = True
                queue.append((neighbor, steps + 1))
        
        # Check prime teleportation
        num = nums[current]
        if num == 1:
            continue
        primes = set()
        if is_prime(num):
            primes.add(num)
        else:
            temp = num
            while temp > 1:
                p = spf[temp]
                primes.add(p)
                while temp % p == 0:
                    temp = temp // p
        for p in primes:
            if p in prime_to_indices:
                for j in prime_to_indices[p]:
                    if j != current and not visited[j]:
                        visited[j] = True
                        queue.append((j, steps + 1))
                # Avoid reprocessing the same prime
                del prime_to_indices[p]
    
    return -1

```ruby
def solve(nums)
  n = nums.size
  return 0 if n == 1

  max_num = nums.max
  spf = Array.new(max_num + 1, 0)
  (2..max_num).each do |i|
    if spf[i] == 0
      spf[i] = i
      (i*i..max_num).step(i) do |j|
        spf[j] = i if spf[j] == 0
      end
    end
  end

  is_prime = lambda do |num|
    return false if num <= 1
    spf[num] == num
  end

  prime_to_indices = {}
  nums.each_with_index do |num, i|
    next if num == 1

    primes = Set.new
    if is_prime.call(num)
      primes.add(num)
    else
      temp = num
      while temp > 1
        p = spf[temp]
        primes.add(p)
        temp /= p while temp % p == 0
      end
    end

    primes.each do |p|
      prime_to_indices[p] ||= []
      prime_to_indices[p] << i
    end
  end

  visited = Array.new(n, false)
  queue = [[0, 0]]
  visited[0] = true

  while !queue.empty?
    current, steps = queue.shift
    return steps if current == n - 1

    [current - 1, current + 1].each do |neighbor|
      if neighbor.between?(0, n - 1) && !visited[neighbor]
        visited[neighbor] = true
        queue << [neighbor, steps + 1]
      end
    end

    num = nums[current]
    next if num == 1

    primes = Set.new
    if is_prime.call(num)
      primes.add(num)
    else
      temp = num
      while temp > 1
        p = spf[temp]
        primes.add(p)
        temp /= p while temp % p == 0
      end
    end

    primes.each do |p|
      if prime_to_indices.key?(p)
        prime_to_indices[p].each do |j|
          if j != current && !visited[j]
            visited[j] = true
            queue << [j, steps + 1]
          end
        end
        prime_to_indices.delete(p)
      end
    end
  end

  -1
end

def solve(nums: list[int]) -> int:
    n = len(nums)
    if n == 1:
        return 0
    
    # Precompute smallest prime factors (SPF) up to the maximum number in nums for sieve
    max_num = max(nums)
    spf = [0] * (max_num + 1)
    for i in range(2, max_num + 1):
        if spf[i] == 0:
            spf[i] = i
            for j in range(i * i, max_num + 1, i):
                if spf[j] == 0:
                    spf[j] = i
    
    def is_prime(num):
        if num <= 1:
            return False
        return spf[num] == num
    
    # Build a map from prime to list of indices divisible by that prime
    prime_to_indices = {}
    for i in range(n):
        num = nums[i]
        if num == 1:
            continue
        primes = set()
        if is_prime(num):
            primes.add(num)
        else:
            # Factorize the number into its prime factors
            temp = num
            while temp > 1:
                p = spf[temp]
                primes.add(p)
                while temp % p == 0:
                    temp = temp // p
        for p in primes:

                prime_to_indices[p] = []
            prime_to_indices[p].append(i)
    
    # BFS setup
    visited = [False] * n
    queue = deque()
    queue.append((0, 0))
    visited[0] = True
    
    while queue:

        if current == n - 1:
            return steps
        
        # Check adjacent moves
        for neighbor in [current - 1, current + 1]:
            if 0 <= neighbor < n and not visited[neighbor]:
                visited[neighbor] = True
                queue.append((neighbor, steps + 1))
        
        # Check prime teleportation
        num = nums[current]
        if num == 1:
            continue
        primes = set()
        if is_prime(num):
            primes.add(num)
        else:
            temp = num
            while temp > 1:
                p = spf[temp]
                primes.add(p)

def solve(nums)
  n = nums.size
  return 0 if n == 1

  max_num = nums.max
  spf = Array.new(max_num + 1, 0)
  (2..max_num).each do |i|
    if spf[i] == 0
      spf[i] = i
      (i*i..max_num).step(i) do |j|
        spf[j] = i if spf[j] == 0
      end
    end
  end

  is_prime = lambda do |num|
    return false if num <= 1
    spf[num] == num
  end

  prime_to_indices = {}
  nums.each_with_index do |num, i|
    next if num == 1

    primes = Set.new
    if is_prime.call(num)
      primes.add(num)
    else
      temp = num
      while temp > 1
        p = spf[temp]
        primes.add(p)
        temp /= p while temp % p == 0
      end
    end

    primes.each do |p|
      prime_to_indices[p] ||= []
      prime_to_indices[p] << i
    end
  end

  visited = Array.new(n, false)
  queue = [[0, 0]]
  visited[0] = true

  while !queue.empty?
    current, steps = queue.shift
    return steps if current == n - 1

    # Check adjacent moves
    [current - 1, current + 1].each do |neighbor|
      if neighbor.between?(0, n - 1) && !visited[neighbor]
        visited[neighbor] = true
        queue << [neighbor, steps + 1]
      end
    end

    # Check prime teleportation
    num = nums[current]
    next if num == 1

    primes = Set.new
    if is_prime.call(num)
      primes.add(num)
    else
      temp = num
      while temp > 1
        p = spf[temp]
        primes.add(p)
        temp /= p while temp % p == 0
      end
    end

    primes.each do |p|
      if prime_to_indices.key?(p)
        prime_to_indices[p].each do |j|
          if j != current && !visited[j]
            visited[j] = true
            queue << [j, steps + 1]
          end
        end
        prime_to_indices.delete(p)
      end
    end
  end

  -1
end
